spec_match.rationale: The header explorer now models the exact v131 asymmetry: source aggregation key identifiers still enforce the 25-character persisted-key cap while trigger source_keys and aggregatable_values accept long descriptive identifiers.

interactive.rationale: Visitors can type or choose key identifiers, edit the source key piece, change the trigger contribution, switch destinations, simulate validation, force the value-budget error, and watch both headers and verdicts update live.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The concept lede and controls now deliver the feature-index promise of a side-by-side source-vs-trigger header explorer showing where the cap still applies and where it does not.

edge_cases.rationale: The demo surfaces source rejection for over-25-character keys, invalid source key pieces, trigger-side acceptance of the same long key, and a separate aggregatable_values budget failure so the removed cap is not overgeneralized.

references.rationale: The page links directly to ChromeStatus feature 5131578140000256, the WICG Attribution Reporting spec section for key identifier length, and the linked spec-change pull request.

design_system.rationale: The boilerplate probe, inline styles, and invisible comparison toggle were removed; the rebuilt page uses design variables, explicit button foreground/background pairs, and stable responsive grids.

summary: Header explorer now answers the previous critique with a real Attribution Reporting header verdict tool, direct reference links, modeled rejection responses, trigger value-budget coverage, and no remaining open questions.
